name: North: Kofta Kebabs in Spicy Cream Sauce (Malai Kofta)
recipeIngredient: Large Russet potatoes, boiled, peeled, mashed
Chick-pea flour or corn flour (see note)
recipeInstructions:
1. Kofta: Combine the mashed potatoes, peas, cheese, chopped green chile, ground coriander, cumin, salt, cashews, and raisins. Form the mixture into 1-inch balls.
2. Make a batter by combining the chickpea flour or corn flour with water (add a pinch of salt if desired).
3. Heat oil to 375 degrees F in a deep fryer or large heavy saucepan. Dip each kofta ball into the batter and then deep-fry until golden brown, about 4 minutes. Remove from oil and set aside.
4. Sauce: Grind cashews, cloves, nutmeg, cinnamon stick, and garlic with a little water to make a fine paste. Set aside.
5. Heat butter in a large saucepan over medium-high heat. Add the grated onion and sauté until wilted. Stir in the ground paste and cook for 2 minutes.
6. Add turmeric, paprika, coriander, cayenne pepper, and salt to the pan. Stir in half-and-half and water. Reduce heat and simmer about 15 minutes.
7. Stir in heavy cream. Bring the sauce to a boil, then remove from heat. Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve immediately over the fried kofta kebabs.
Note: Chick-pea flour (also called besan) may be purchased at Indian grocery stores.
